name: Uptime Kuma
what: Self-hosted uptime monitoring and status page tool for HTTP, TCP, ping, and application health endpoints.
solves: Basic uptime checks, certificate expiry alerts, and operator-facing status visibility without standing up a heavier observability stack first.
users: Ops and engineering teams that need lightweight service monitoring and status pages.
fit: Good fit as a low-friction operational layer for early rollout, especially while the stack is still expanding and formal observability is being layered in.
runtime: Node.js application with local/attached storage and outbound notification integrations.
components: Web UI, monitor definitions, notification channels, persistent storage, and optional public status page.
deploy: Docker is the normal path; single-node deployment is common.
deps: Persistent volume, outbound notification channel, reverse proxy, and backup of monitor configuration.
datastores: Internal application storage (SQLite or configured database depending on deployment mode).
auth: Local users; protect with reverse proxy/TLS and strong credentials.
apis: HTTP checks, notifications, status page URLs, and import/export features.
security: Keep the admin UI private, patch regularly, and avoid exposing internal-only endpoints publicly through status pages.
setup_path: Deploy via official container, reverse-proxy behind TLS, configure notifications, and import monitors for all tier-0 services.
env: Port, base URL, reverse-proxy headers, persistence path, and notification credentials.
persistence: Back up monitor config and internal database/volume.
operations: Add monitors as new apps go live, test notifications, and patch on release cadence.
failure_points: Treating it as a full observability platform, forgetting backups, or exposing sensitive internal target details on public status pages.
integration: Useful immediately for public site, APIs, Kong, auth endpoints, key app front doors, and certificate checks.
verify: Decide whether Hylono wants a public status page or strictly internal monitoring.
risk: Low. Lightweight and easy to replace if observability matures.
gdpr: Minimal direct data risk unless notifications or public pages expose user data.
overlap: Overlaps slightly with Prometheus/Grafana alerting but provides faster operator value for basic uptime checks.
codex_read_first: Read the deployment docs and decide which endpoints should be monitored privately versus exposed on a status page.
codex_best_path: Use it as the first visibility layer while Prometheus/Grafana mature underneath.
codex_never_assume: Never assume Uptime Kuma replaces metrics, traces, logs, or security monitoring.
